# Transcrição: 01 Rapid Fire Testing - Round #3

- **Fonte:** 01 Rapid Fire Testing - Round #3
- **Duração:** 09:45
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Alright guys, so let's go into our third round, a third and final round I should say, of rapid fire testing and that is where we're basically testing out the creative. I'm going to show you inside the next video on the optimization side of this and you're going to be amazed at how much of a difference this round actually makes. It's one of my favorite rounds actually in rapid fire testing for e-commerce. So in this round we're basically going through three faces or three separate rounds in itself. The first one is where we're going to test out imagery or thumbnails. So if you have a video that's really doing well, you're really going to be testing out the thumbnail here otherwise you're just going to be testing out different kinds of visuals for the product offer that you have already spotted or identified in round two. The second round in this phase is going to be testing out the headlines. So we're going to be trying to see different headlines such as using product descriptions or hooks. Which one of those angles is going to work best for us? And then finally the third one is where we test out the copy. So do we have emojis in the copy? What impact does long form do? Short form, mid form, etc. etc. And what will happen at the end of this rapid fire testing round is you're going to end up with a piece of creative that's getting you really good results. So extremely good CTRs, extremely good CPCs. And then ongoing, as you continue to work on this account, as you continue to work with this client, this is kind of the round that you'll mostly be spending your time with continuously testing creative and rolling out fresh creative to make sure that you don't get ad fatigue in that account. This round is actually something that's perpetually happening inside the ad account. Now when you end up with that piece of creative that is getting you really good CTRs, really cheap ad to carts, really cheap purchases, etc. etc. It's also an idea to use that creative and go back into maybe some of the personas and audiences that when you first tested them out in the first rapid fire round didn't work. It's worth actually rolling it out again because maybe it was just the creative that didn't appeal to that audience at that time or that persona. So I would always go back and test that winning creative on some of the audiences that maybe didn't perform so well the first time. But the audiences that you're going to be testing this round on are going to be page fans so you're going to have an ad set for page fans. And then you're going to have an ad set for people that visited the site in the last 60 days but create an exclusion of anyone that's still inside of the retargeting sequence. So usually that's about 30 days.

## [03:00 - 06:00]

If they don't have any website traffic that you can create that custom audience from just use a similar look like or a similar audience to the one that did end up working for you that is currently running in the conversion objective campaign. So when we're testing out images you'll see in the second video where I take you through the optimization really this is pretty straightforward. You're just testing out different thumbnails different images that the client has of that product. You're even testing out maybe adding a copy to the image not having copied to the image what impact does that do to the CTRs right. When we're testing headlines which is the second phase of this round usually things that we would test out here is we would test out product description. For example here the Carolina dress that would be the headline. It would literally be the description of the product page. We would test out the hook so maybe they have free shipping or after pay or maybe there's 50% off for example. We would test out a headline where it's more around the lifestyle or the feelings around that. So this is the cutest little summer dress or she's going to look so cute in this dress for example or your child will be the most stylish child on the block. So really playing on those emotions. And we would also test out urgency and authority. So for example if they had been reviewed in a magazine like Elle we would say you know as voted number one in Elle magazine or as voted best dressed by other moms. It's good to also test out a combo between having just the headline no link descriptions and then also testing out once you identify the winning headline here I would quickly just go and test out you know what impact does it have if I add the price in the link description or if I add the hook in the link description or if I add you know something else like available in different colors. Does that increase the CTRs even more? Does that actually hurt the CTRs? So this is the approach we would take to this. And then finally the third round inside of this you know third rapid fire testing round is we test out the copy. So now we have the winning image the winning visuals. We know the headlines that are working the best. Obviously with headlines this is assuming that it wasn't a single image or a single video that worked well. If that's the case obviously you're bypassing this phase altogether. The last phase is testing the copy and typically here the approach you would take is testing out you know having emojis versus not having emojis. Having lots of links versus not that many links. Having a shortened link versus a longer link. Having a long form post versus a short form and having mid form etc.

## [06:00 - 09:00]

You're basically trying to hack your existing creative to see how can we get results like how can we get a results or cost per results down even more. And you'll see in the next video that it is absolutely stunning the stuff that you can do with this kind of testing. So you'll have this super piece of creative at the end of the day. Now once you identify that winning piece of creative I wouldn't just you know you need to pull that into your conversion objective campaign obviously. But I wouldn't just turn off this post engagement. I would continue to run it to build some of that social proof in it. So keep running it until you see a little bit of fatigue happening inside of that post engagement objective campaign. Now for those of you testing that copy just some of the best practices here it's good to link to the site or the product page. So always make sure you have a link inside that creative. I would also. Now here's what you're ultimately looking for in each of those phases. You're looking for ultimately purchases and add to cart. Those are great but they may not always happen. What you want to look at is your CTR so your link click CTR and your link click cost per click so CPC. You want to see which one has a really good link click CTR and which one has a really low link click CPC and ultimately ideally those would have translated into add to carts as well as purchases. But another important stat to look at here is your landing page views because sometimes you may find you have an ad that has a huge CTR really cheap cost per click but the landing page views are abysmal relative to the amount of clicks you've sent meaning that ad may be a little bit too click baity. People are clicking it and then they didn't realize they were clicking it and they just bounce right and so that's not going to be good for anyone long term. So you want to look at everything holistically. You want to make sure that people that are clicking they're actually landing on the website and waiting for it to load and then obviously if you're getting add to carts and purchases that's something to consider as well. So this is the third phase of our rapid fire testing is where we test out our creative. So we're testing copy headlines if headlines are applicable to you and also the images or the thumbnails whatever you're using there as your creative. When you're done with this you're going to have a super mega amazing powerful piece of copy that you then pull into your conversion objective campaign and you'll continue to run that piece of copy as well in your post engagement objective campaign to build up some social proof.

## [09:00 - 09:45]

Now as you go on with the account as you keep managing this account this is what you're this phase so this third phase of rapid fire testing is what you're going to perpetually be doing. You're going to be constantly testing out new creatives to those audiences to see to have things prepared in the background so that when your conversion objective starts to show shine signs of ad fatigue you've already got some really amazing creative ready to go that's social proof that's on standby so that you're not delaying purchases or scalability or results for your client and for yourself potentially as well.
